response:
Alex reads books.
Books change views.
Views shift slowly.
Slowly time passes.
Passes days quickly.
Quickly pages turn.
Turn pages daily.
Daily counts matter.
Matter most here.
Here math helps.
Helps find rates.
Rates show speed.
Speed equals pages.
Pages per day.
Day one had.
Had twelve pages.
Twelve times ten.
Ten times four.
Four days spent.
Spent six days.
Days for second.
Second had thirty.
Thirty times six.
Six times twenty.
Twenty times eight.
Eight days used.
Used all time.
Time sums up.
Up to three.
Three novels total.
Total pages read.
Read daily average.
Average is twenty.
Twenty pages daily.
